diagrams.title: Firewall Types Classification Hierarchy
diagrams.description: A hierarchical tree diagram showing the classification of firewall types from basic packet filtering to advanced next-generation firewalls, illustrating the evolution and complexity of firewall technologies.
elements: Proxy Firewall (branch 3) - Application-level Proxy, Circuit-level Proxy
Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) (branch 4) - Deep Packet Inspection, Intrusion Prevention, Application Awareness
diagrams.purpose: Shows the hierarchical relationship between different firewall types, helping learners understand how firewall technologies have evolved from simple packet filtering to sophisticated next-generation solutions with advanced features.

diagrams.title: Firewall Traffic Filtering Process
diagrams.description: A flow diagram illustrating how a firewall processes incoming network traffic, showing the decision-making process based on security rules, and demonstrating the allow/deny logic with visual paths.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the traffic filtering mechanism of firewalls, demonstrating how security rules control network access and the default deny principle, which is fundamental to understanding firewall operation.

diagrams.title: Firewall Deployment Architecture
diagrams.description: A network topology diagram showing multiple firewall placement points in an organizational network, illustrating perimeter, internal (DMZ), and host-based firewalls creating defense in depth.
elements: Internal Firewall (between DMZ and internal network)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates defense-in-depth architecture by showing how multiple firewall layers work together at different network segments, helping security professionals understand proper firewall placement for comprehensive network protection.
